title: Support checking access qualifier for `typedef` inside class
body:
Hello!

Verilator allows adding an access qualifier to a `typedef` inside a class, but does not react in any way if it is violated.

The expected behavior is one of two things:

* PREFERRED (standard?): prohibit adding access qualifier - as far as I understand, section "8.18 Data hiding and encapsulation" (IEEE 1800-2023) allows access qualifiers to be applied only to class properties and methods
* ALTERNATIVE (non-standard?): Verilator throws an error when access is violated

**Example:**

```systemverilog
module top;
  class cls;
    local typedef bit t1;
    protected typedef bit t2;
  endclass

  cls::t1 var1; // access error expected but there is none
  cls::t2 var2; // access error expected but there is none
endmodule
```

**Output:**

Normal output without error. Nothing interesting :)

**Command:**

```bash
verilator --binary top.sv
```

---

Verilator version (git master): `Verilator 5.029 devel rev v5.028-88-g195a66529`

OS (verilator devcontainer): Ubuntu 22.04.4 LTS
